Content: 2. The extruder should have a similar pattern of temperature settings as for pipe extrusion (type I). 3. Blown film molding can be carried out by both an air and a water cooling system. But TPX has a narrower process window than PP and PE due to lower melt viscosity. <--- Page Split --->

### d. SHEET EXTRUSION  

1. Sheet extrusion of TPX is carried out easily. MX004 (medium rigidity) and MX002 (flexible grade) have excellent processability. 2. The temperature setting for sheet molding is similar to that for pipe extrusion(type I). 3. On sheet extrusion, the temperature of the chill roll should be in the range of 40 to \(80^{\circ}\mathrm{C}\) to get good contact. 4. In order to aid good contact between a chill roll and the molten film, equipment such as an air knife, air chamber and electrification equipment can be used. 5. Since TPX has low melt viscosity at the molding temperature, the vertical extrusion die which is generally used for polymers such as polyesters and polyamides is recommendable for TPX extrusion. ![](images/8_0.jpg)
  

### e. PROFILE EXTRUSION  

1. MX002 and MX004 is suitable for profile extrusion from the point of processability. 2. The extruder should have a similar pattern of temperature settings as that for pipe extrusion(type I). 3. Since TPX has low melt viscosity, shortening the distance between a die and the sizing equipment makes the molding stable. 4. When hollow products are molded by profile extrusion, a heater should be installed to the inner die to get products with smooth inner surface as with pipe extrusion. <--- Page Split --->

a. When TPX is extruded, a screw with a water cooling system should not be used because it obstructs plasticization of TPX. b. When a general-purpose full flight screw is used for TPX, the output of TPX is smaller than that of PP or PE. Preheating of TPX pellets is sometimes effective to recover the decrease. According to claim 1, the third PE layer (6) includes a fourth PE layer (61) and a fifth PE layer (62), and the sum of the thicknesses of the fourth PE layer (61) and the fifth PE layer (62) is 230μm, 250μm, 260μm, or 280μm. 10. According to claim 1, the PE material in the first PE layer (2), the second PE layer (4), and the third PE layer (6) can be any one or a combination of ultra-low density polyethylene, low-density polyethylene, and linear low-density polyethylene. ## A Composite Film Suitable for Manufacturing Cell Culture Containers Technical Field

[0001] This practical new-type belongs to the field of biopharmaceuticals, specifically involving a composite film suitable for manufacturing cell culture containers. ## Background Technology

[0002] Multilayer composite films, also known as co-extruded/laminated films, are plastic films composed of two or more layers of different materials that are co-extruded or laminated together. They are mainly used in the manufacture of flexible containers for biopharmaceuticals. Through co-extrusion/lamination, a material with comprehensive properties of individual materials can be obtained. For different application scenarios, suitable materials are selected for co-extrusion/lamination, which is one of the key factors in designing composite films. Additionally, the thickness of each layer and the arrangement order of single-layer membranes are two other key factors that affect the final physical and chemical properties and biological safety of the composite film. [0003] In biopharmaceutical processes, high-molecular-weight plastic film materials are used to manufacture inner liners of bio-reactors. The film material comes into contact with the culture medium for a long time, causing various chemicals in the film material to migrate into the culture medium. Some of these chemicals are antioxidants added during the film formatio...

Content: 50 To check the influence of c-beam exposure to heat resistance of the blends, dynamic mechanical analysis (DMA) is performed in the temperature range of about —80° C. to about 200° C. This test can determine the glass transition temperature, melting point of a thermoplastic by following changes in ss viscoelastic behavior of a material with temperature. In a typical DMA test, the storage modulus measures how stiff and elastic the material is, the loss modulus indicates how fluid-like and viscous the material is and the loss tangent is the ratio of loss modulus to storage modulus. For a polymer 60 material to exhibit some heat-resistance so that no deformation occurs under its own weight when exposed to elevated temperatures, the storage modulus of the material is typically at least above about 1 MPa, while the loss tangent value is typically lower than about 0.25 MPa. Using these criteria, the 65 maximum temperature that a thermoplastic elastomer material can be exposed to for short time, such as a steam sterilization process, can be estimated. In a particular embodiment, the polyolefin plastomer or the polyolefin elastomer is polyethylene based, polypropylene based, or combination thereof. For instance, the polyolefin has a density of less than 0.915 g/cc and the polyolefin is, for instance, a very low density polyethylene (VLDPE), a polyolefin plastomer (POP), a polyolefin elastomer (POE) based on polyethylene and polypropylene, or combination thereof. The vacuum chamber 22 can simply consist of the cylindrical housing whose inner diameter closely conforms to the 5 outer diameter of the inflated tubing. A vacuum can be applied through outlet 30 by a conventional vacuum hose.
